payload.claim_text: New mental health co-response teams: NYC clinicians & MTAPD working together. Focus 100% on the most troubled, most troubling clients: acutely psychotic and/or deeply entrenched. 'Help right now' approach.

SCOUT key elements: Clinician-led (Engage, De-escalate, Encourage voluntary acceptance of services); Police for Safety & Support (All officers receive NYS OMH crisis intervention training); True Teams (Same officers & same clinicians, day in & day out); 'Help Right Now' Mindset (Focus on hardest cases); Use All Available Powers (Voluntary outcomes preferred, involuntary transport where appropriate); Continuing Care (Work with hospital & shelter partners).

Today: two teams in Manhattan. 2024: three more teams in Bronx, Brooklyn, Queens. 2025: up to ten teams. NYS funding will cover more nurses & social workers, MTAPD officers, program oversight & assessment.
